Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Memo: A language that remembers only the last 12 lines of code 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 429 upvotes, 95 comments
  3. George Goble has died 99 upvotes, 19 comments
  4. Foxing aspires to be an eBPF-powered replication engine for Linux filesystems 34 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1079 upvotes, 331 comments
  6. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 232 upvotes, 64 comments
  7. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 415 upvotes, 143 comments
  8. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 57 upvotes, 15 comments
  9. LinkedIn is searching your browser extensions 1538 upvotes, 676 comments
  10. Prefer do notation over Applicative operators when assembling records (2024) 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  11. JSON Canvas Spec (2024) 81 upvotes, 28 comments
  12. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 115 upvotes, 46 comments
  13. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 247 upvotes, 112 comments
  14. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 732 upvotes, 376 comments
  15. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 137 upvotes, 59 comments
  16. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 167 upvotes, 75 comments
  17. Tailscale's new macOS home 291 upvotes, 147 comments
  18. Significant raise of reports 274 upvotes, 145 comments
  19. Show HN: Made a little Artemis II tracker 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. Queueing Requests Queues Your Capacity Problems, Too 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  21. A Few Good Magazines From the 70s and 80s 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Every Law a Commit – US Law in GitHub 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. Magic the Gathering Deck Shuffler 30 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 295 upvotes, 222 comments
  25. The Australian government has announced gambling advertising reforms 83 upvotes, 53 comments
  26. Maze Algorithms 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Cursor 3 264 upvotes, 228 comments
  28. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 137 upvotes, 123 comments
  29. The Beginning of Programming as We'll Know It 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. ParadeDB (YC S23) Is Hiring Database Internal Engineers (Rust) 0 upvotes, 0 comments
